title: Threshold Session Deferred (3.7 km Evening Run)

sections.markdown:
- 2 km easy warm-up
- 3 x 8 minutes at threshold effort with 2 minutes easy jog between reps
- Cool down easy to 10 km total

- This was supposed to be the week's first threshold session, but the actual run was only `3.73 km` in `26:21`, so the planned workout was not completed on Tuesday.
- Average heart rate stayed moderate for a short hilly run, which supports the read that this became more of a reset or leg-loosener than any meaningful threshold stimulus.

- The key short-term planning point was to avoid pretending this counted as Tuesday quality. It did not.
- Once this became a substitute day, the week's threshold stimulus needed to move to Wednesday, `2026-04-01`, and the rest of the week had to be adjusted around that shift.

- Missing a threshold day is not a major problem by itself. The bigger issue is whether missed quality sessions get acknowledged honestly and rescheduled cleanly instead of being half-counted.
- In the context of this block, this note matters because it preserves the correct training read: Tuesday was a lighter day, not hidden threshold work.

- Apple Health recorded `240` during-run heart-rate samples. Average HR was `146.4 bpm`, ranging from `88.0` to `162.0 bpm`.
- First-half average HR was `141.7 bpm` and second-half average HR was `150.1 bpm` (+8.3 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Apple Health cadence averaged `158.4 spm`, with a working range of `102.0` to `180.0 spm`.
- First-half cadence averaged `158.8 spm` and second-half cadence averaged `157.9 spm` (-0.9 spm), which looks mechanically stable.

- Strava moving pace averaged `7:04 /km` across `3.729 km` in `26:21` moving time.
- Estimated first-half moving pace was `6:53 /km` and second-half pace was `7:16 /km` (+23 s/km), so pace faded meaningfully in the back half.

- Strava summary HR came in at `145.4 bpm` average and `162 bpm` max.
- Distance-split HR averaged `140.6 bpm` in the first half and `149.8 bpm` in the second (+9.2 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Moving time was `26:21` out of `26:24` elapsed (99.8%), which kept stoppage cost negligible.
- The downsampled moving stream shows `6` stop segments and `0.6%` stationary samples, so the run was mostly continuous with a few interruptions.

- Strava logged about `84.8 m` of climbing, with altitude moving between roughly `150.2 m` and `184.8 m`.
- Estimated climbing was `42.2 m` in the first half and `42.6 m` in the second, so the climbing load was split fairly evenly.
